REGIONS.name: The Apprentice & the Bracelet
REGIONS.tagline: A forgotten sword, a sorcerer who paints people, and a power that flattens you into the wall.

steps.t: You're the blacksmith's apprentice in Kakariko Village, and you've overslept. Head into the smithy; the smith hands you the captain's newly forged sword and tells you to deliver it to Hyrule Castle at once.

items.name: Captain's Sword
items.note: Your starting blade — the sword you're delivering. Swing with the attack button; at full hearts it fires a beam. (You'll trade up to the Master Sword later.)

steps.t: This is the tutorial stretch — explore Kakariko, talk to people, and cut grass for Rupees. Your real power comes in a few minutes.
Leave Kakariko and travel to Hyrule Castle to the north. Deliver the sword to the captain at the gate.
steps.stuck: From Kakariko, follow the road north across Hyrule Field to the castle. Talk to the soldier/captain near the entrance to hand over the sword.

steps.t: A commotion sends you toward the Sanctuary to the east — the priest and a young woman named Seres are in danger. Hurry there.
Inside the Sanctuary you find the sorcerer Yuga — he can seal living people into PAINTINGS on the wall. Before your eyes he turns Seres into a portrait and peels her off the wall.
Chase Yuga and swing at him. He's untouchable for now — he presses YOU flat against the wall as a painting and you black out.
You come to back home in Kakariko, saved and confused. Head downstairs — someone has moved into your house.

sections.name: Ravio's Bracelet
sections.sub: The power to merge — and the quest begins

steps.t: A hooded merchant named Ravio has set up a rental shop in your house. In thanks for the lodging he gives you a strange Bracelet — the source of the wall-merging power.

items.name: Ravio's Bracelet
items.note: Press its button to MERGE into a wall as a flat moving painting — slip through grates, around corners, and onto far ledges. The mechanic the whole game is built around.

steps.t: Try it out: stand against a wall and use the Bracelet to press flat into it, then slide along the wall to reach the other side. Step off the wall to pop back out.
steps.stuck: Face a flat wall, hold/press the Bracelet button, and Link becomes a painting on the surface. Move left/right along the wall (you can't leave it), then release at an opening or ledge to step back into the world. Merging drains the green energy meter, which refills fast.

steps.t: Ravio's Shop now RENTS you most of the game's items cheaply (Bow, Bombs, Hammer, Hookshot, rods…), in almost any order. Catch: if you die, his bird repossesses your rentals until you re-rent — so BUY your favorites once you can afford it.
Visit the elder Sahasrahla (follow the marker). He explains Yuga's plot to revive Ganon and tells you the realm's hope lies in the Master Sword — which needs the three Pendants of Virtue.
steps.stuck: Sahasrahla's house is in Kakariko; the game marks it. Talk to him fully to set your goal and open the way east.

steps.t: Your path is set: recover the three Pendants of Virtue. Start with the Pendant of Courage in the Eastern Palace, off to the east of Hyrule Field — rent any item you fancy from Ravio first.

RUNES.name: Ravio's Bracelet
RUNES.from: Given by Ravio, the merchant who sets up shop in your house early in the game.
RUNES.what: Press to MERGE into a wall as a flat moving painting — slip through barred grates, around corners, and across to ledges you can't walk to. The power that defines the whole game.
RUNES.tip: Merging drains your green energy meter (it refills fast when you step out). If a path looks blocked, ask 'can I merge along this wall?' first.

RUNES.name: Master Sword
RUNES.from: Drawn from its pedestal in the Lost Woods once you hold all three Pendants of Virtue.
RUNES.what: The legendary blade — stronger than your borrowed sword, and it fires a sword beam when your hearts are full.
RUNES.tip: It's also the key that lets you pass safely into Lorule's deepest places. Get all three Pendants first.

RUNES.name: Bow
RUNES.from: Rented or bought from Ravio's Shop.
RUNES.what: Fires arrows at distant switches, eyes, and enemies; arrows draw from your energy meter, so you never run out of ammo.
RUNES.tip: Most items in this game cost ENERGY, not consumable ammo — fire freely, just watch the green meter.

RUNES.name: Pegasus Boots
RUNES.from: Found in Hyrule (a gift after an early errand) — not rented from Ravio.
RUNES.what: Hold to dash in a straight line, smashing through some objects and knocking enemies back.
RUNES.tip: Dash + merge combos open several shortcuts and Maiamai nooks.

RUNES.name: Tornado Rod
RUNES.from: Rented or bought from Ravio's Shop.
RUNES.what: Whips up a gust that flings Link into the air (and flips nearby enemies) — reach high ledges and stun shielded foes.
RUNES.tip: Great for crossing gaps and for getting the drop on armored enemies.

RUNES.name: Hookshot
RUNES.from: Rented or bought from Ravio's Shop.
RUNES.what: Fires a chain that grabs posts and pulls you across gaps, and stuns or pulls enemies.
RUNES.tip: Pairs with wall-merge for some of the trickiest dungeon traversals.

CHAMPIONS.name: Pendant of Courage
CHAMPIONS.note: Won by clearing the Eastern Palace — one of the three Pendants of Virtue needed for the Master Sword.

CHAMPIONS.name: Pendant of Wisdom
CHAMPIONS.note: Won by clearing the House of Gales out on the lake — the second Pendant of Virtue.

CHAMPIONS.name: Pendant of Power
CHAMPIONS.note: Won atop the Tower of Hera on Death Mountain — the third Pendant. With all three, draw the Master Sword.

CHAMPIONS.name: Sage Gulley
CHAMPIONS.note: Free the painted Sage Gulley from the Dark Palace — one of the seven Sages you must rescue across Lorule.

CHAMPIONS.name: Sage Oren
CHAMPIONS.note: Free Sage Oren, the Zora queen, from the Swamp Palace.

CHAMPIONS.name: Sage Seres
CHAMPIONS.note: Free Sage Seres from the Skull Woods in Lorule's dark forest.

CHAMPIONS.name: Sage Osfala
CHAMPIONS.note: Free Sage Osfala from the Thieves' Hideout (with Thief Girl's help).

CHAMPIONS.name: Sage Rosso
CHAMPIONS.note: Free Sage Rosso from the frozen Ice Ruins.

CHAMPIONS.name: Sage Irene
CHAMPIONS.note: Free Sage Irene from the Desert Palace in the Lorule sands.

CHAMPIONS.name: Sage Impa
CHAMPIONS.note: Free Sage Impa from Turtle Rock — the seventh Sage opens the way to Lorule Castle.

ROADMAP.name: The Three Pendants
ROADMAP.note: Courage, Wisdom, and Power — won from the Eastern Palace, the House of Gales, and the Tower of Hera. Carry all three to the Lost Woods pedestal to draw the Master Sword.

ROADMAP.name: The Seven Sages
ROADMAP.note: Yuga has sealed seven Sages into paintings across Lorule's dungeons. Free all seven to open Lorule Castle and confront Yuga Ganon.

ROADMAP.name: Mother Maiamai's 100 Babies
ROADMAP.note: One hundred baby Maiamai are hidden across Hyrule and Lorule (often behind walls you must merge into). Return them to Mother Maiamai to upgrade your items into stronger 'Nice' versions.

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Twenty-eight are hidden across both worlds. Every four extend your life by a full heart.

TIPS.name: Merge into the walls
items: Ravio's Bracelet lets you press flat into a wall and slide along it as a moving painting — through barred grates, around corners, and onto ledges you can't walk to. It's the answer to most 'how do I get there?' puzzles.
Merging spends your green energy meter; it refills almost instantly when you step back out, so merge often.

You also cross between Hyrule and Lorule by MERGING into the purple wall fissures (and later via the warp paintings). Lorule is the same map, twisted — a wall you can't pass in one world may be open in the other.

TIPS.name: Ravio's rental shop
items: Unlike older Zeldas, you don't find your tools in dungeons — Ravio's Shop (in your house) RENTS you almost every item (Bow, Hammer, Bombs, Hookshot, Tornado Rod, Fire/Ice Rod, Sand Rod…) cheaply, up front, in almost any order.
The catch: if you DIE, Ravio's bird repossesses every rented item until you re-rent them. Once you can afford it, BUY your favorites so a death can't disarm you.
Because items aren't gated, you can tackle the dungeons in a fairly free order — rent what a dungeon needs and go.

TIPS.name: Energy, not ammo
items: Items don't use consumable ammo here — the Bow, rods, Hammer, Bombs and merging all drain one shared GREEN energy meter that recharges on its own.
So fire and merge freely; just pace yourself in a fight so you don't bottom the meter out mid-combo.
Pieces of Heart raise life; the meter grows a little as you progress and via some upgrades.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a full Heart Container.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Maiamai
COLLECTIBLES.note: Hidden across Hyrule & Lorule (many behind merge-walls); return them to Mother Maiamai for 'Nice' item upgrades.
